meta.problem: We're a broke veteran-crisis nonprofit. We need funding we don't have to pay back — grants, foundations, corporate giving — found and teed up for application.

agent.name: suas-grant-finder
agent.model_rationale: Newest Opus-class for careful, verified web research where a hallucinated grant wastes the team's time. A run is on-demand and costs cents; Sonnet is the cheaper fallback if cost matters more than depth (see NEXT-DIRECTIONS).
agent.system_summary: Fundraising/grants research expert for SUAS. Searches the web for real, currently-open grants/foundations/corporate-giving for veteran crisis, mental-health, suicide-prevention and QRF-type work; verifies each by fetching the source; ranks by fit/$/deadline; writes /mnt/session/outputs/grant-report.md with a ranked table + drafted letters of inquiry for the top 3.
never_dos: Never contact, email, or submit anything to anyone — research and drafting only
Never invent a funder, link, award amount, or deadline; every entry must come from a page actually fetched, with its URL
Anything that can't be verified from a fetched source is omitted or marked 'unverified'

outcome.description: Produce a decision-ready, verified grant-prospect report as grant-report.md
rubric_criteria: grant-report.md exists and lists at least 8 currently-open funding opportunities relevant to veteran crisis / mental-health / suicide-prevention / emergency-response work
Each opportunity includes funder, award amount or range, deadline (or 'rolling'), eligibility summary, a one-line 'why it fits SUAS', and a working application/source URL the agent actually retrieved
The top 3 best-fit opportunities each include a tailored drafted letter of inquiry / outreach email of 250 words or fewer
No funder, link, deadline, or amount is fabricated; anything unverifiable from a fetched source is omitted or explicitly marked 'unverified'
Opportunities are ranked by fit + award size + deadline proximity, with a one-line ranking rationale

connections.notes: v0 uses public web only and drafts (never sends). Roadmap: an email/Gmail connector + vault credential, gated always_ask, so the agent can send the LOIs after human approval (NEXT-DIRECTIONS v2).

memory.notes: No memory store in v0 (on-demand, full list each run). v1 adds a 'grants-seen' store so a recurring scan reports only NEW opportunities.

schedule.human: Run it whenever you want a fresh sweep. v1 puts it on a monthly cron.

cases.name: case-01
cases.expected: evals/case-01/expected.md (filled with the first verified good run)

evals.notes: No golden set yet — the first verified report becomes the regression baseline.

defaults_applied.write_actions: None in v0 — the agent drafts, humans send

interface.how: Report lands as grant-report.md (download from the session). Console for run history.

next_directions.what: Run it monthly on a schedule, reporting only NEW grants since last time
next_directions.why_deferred: v0 is on-demand to prove the output first; scheduling is a deliberate v1 upgrade
next_directions.how: Create a scheduled deployment (monthly cron + timezone, same kickoff as initial_events) + a 'grants-seen' memory store attached read_write; the agent diffs against it each run

next_directions.what: Let the agent send the top LOIs itself after you approve each one
next_directions.why_deferred: Write-action to an external system — kept human-in-the-loop and drafts-only for v0
next_directions.how: Add an email/Gmail MCP connector + a vault credential; gate the toolset always_ask so Jacob approves each send

next_directions.what: Clone this expert into marketing, brand, and donor-appeal experts under one coordinator (the full-automation vision)
next_directions.why_deferred: Out of scope for the first iteration — prove one expert end-to-end first
next_directions.how: Build each specialist as its own agent with a scoped system prompt, then a multiagent coordinator (type: coordinator) that delegates to the roster

next_directions.what: Switch the model to Sonnet-class if run cost matters more than research depth
next_directions.why_deferred: Opus chosen for verification quality; cost is cents either way at on-demand frequency
next_directions.how: agents update with model claude-sonnet-4-6 (or fast Opus {"id":"claude-opus-4-8","speed":"fast"}); re-run evals/case-01 before trusting it

next_directions.what: Re-run evals/case-01 before promoting any new agent version
next_directions.why_deferred: Process habit, not a build task
next_directions.how: Run evals/run-evals.sh against the new version; only promote when the verdict holds
